rulings.Case Title: JESSICA JIMENEZ VS. MENZIES AVIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 29, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JESSICA JIMENEZ'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. Plaintiff proposes to allege an entirely new theory of recovery regarding a new airport. Plaintiff seeks to change the tenor of the case, which has been pending for years. Allowing the amendment will lead to further discovery, delay, and prejudice to defendant. Lastly, Plaintiff admits that it can file a new action regarding the proposed claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRAVIN PATEL et al VS. SHARAD SHANTILAL PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Renewed Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 29, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFFS PRAVIN PATEL AND GOVINDJI PATEL'S Motion For Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Renewed Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion for Fees and Costs is DENIED. The Court finds Defendant's motion for reconsideration was submitted in a timely manner and that Defendant complied in good faith with CCP Sec. 425.16(c) as well as CCP Sec. 128.7. See CCP Sec. 128.7 every pleading, petition, written notice of motion, or other similar paper must meet the following: (1) It is not being presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation. (2) The claims, defenses, and other legal contentions therein are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law. (3) The allegations and other factual contentions have evidentiary support or, if specifically so identified, are likely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery. (4) The denials of factual contentions are warranted on the evidence or, if specifically so identified, are reasonably based on a lack of information or belief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SERGEY KHOLOPOV et al VS. IRINA LITVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 29, 2015, Line 3, CROSS-DEFENDANTS SERGEY KHOLOPOV AND SIARHEI AHANESIAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's Demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Complaint (FAC) is not uncertain, ambiguous or unintelligible. The elements of a conversion claim are: (1) Plaintiff's ownership or right to possession of the property; (2) Defendant's conversion by a wrongful act or disposition of property right; and (3) damages. (See Cal. Civ. Jury Instructions 2100.) The FAC adequately sets forth the ultimate facts necessary to establish each element of the conversion cause of action. (See FAC at 10 - 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT, JASON BISHOP, MIRA BLANCHARD, JOHN BRENNAN'S Motion For Issue Sanctions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Plaintiffs Daniel Barrett ("Barrett"), and Mary O'Shea ("Shea"), collectively referred to here as the "Plaintiffs," served Requests for Admission ("RFA") on Defendant Real Bright Media, Inc. ("RBM") on August 18, 2014. RBM subsequently served timely responses and amended responses. Plaintiffs move for issue sanctions, deeming the RFAs admitted and for monetary sanctions. Usually issue sanctions are only available upon violation of order of the court, but in the case of RFAs they may be "deemed admitted" if respondent fails to make a timely response. Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2033.280. "RFAs are not to be deemed admitted unless the party to whom RFAs are propounded fails to respond prehearing to RFAs in a manner that is substantially Code-compliant (§ 2033.280, subd. (c)), or he or she is recalcitrant and violates a court order compelling further responses that are deficient." St. Mary v. Superior Court, 223 Cal. App. 4th 762, 784 (2014). (see part 2 of 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Mary O'Shea, Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan, Brian Gregor, Erik Hellman, Linda Hernandez, Derek Hill, Carol Meiner, Luci Seed, Tyler Zahn Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verifications And To Deem Rfas Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Mary O'Shea, Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan, Brian Gregor, Erik Hellman, Linda Hernandez, Derek Hill, Carol Meiner, Luci Seed, Tyler Zahn's Motion To Compel Verifications And To Deem Rfas Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Posting Of Tentative Ruling). Thus to adjudicate the question of sanctions, the Court still must determine the validity of the Motion and/or whether the Opposition was substantially justified. See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2023.030 ("If a monetary sanction is authorized by any provision of this title, the court shall impose that sanction unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust."). To complicate matters, the record here is both voluminous, and in some cases, self-contradictory. Some of the verifications at issue, for example, are blank, contain multiple signature dates, are signed on days that post-date the attached proof of service, and according to Plaintiffs at least, may be misnamed, i.e., RBM's Second Amended Response to the Barr RFA may in fact be the "first" amended response. The Court declines to wade through the record in its current state, trying to make sense of it and instead ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: Prior to the hearing, Parties shall meet and confer and shall jointly draft/approve a spreadsheet, entitled "JOINT SPECIAL SEPARATE STATEMENT RE MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFICATION" listing, in rows, each and every verification, alleged verification, and/or missing verification for each and every discovery document at issue on the Instant Motion. Columns shall demarcate dates when: (1) the verification was executed; (2) the verification was served (and manner of service); and (3) citation to the record where the Court can find this evidence. The Parties shall submit the Joint Special Separate Statement to the Court before the Hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan'S Motion For Issue Sanctions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Psoting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: et for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan'S Motion For Issue Sanctions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Psoting Of Tentative Ruling). Here there is some controversy raised in a parallel Motion as to whether the RFAs were timely verified. "As caselaw provides that an unverified [RFA] response is tantamount to no response at all." Melendrez v. Superior Court of the State of California, 215 Cal. App. 4th 1343, 1348 (2013). But Plaintiffs conceded in parallel Reply on the Verification Motion that verifications have been filed. See Pl. Reply to Def. Opp. to Compel Verifications and Deem RFA Admitted, at 6:14-15, Apr. 22, 2015. But this does not end the controversy. Under the Discovery Act, "the court is required to grant a deemed admission motion" where respondents fail to serve a verified response "before the hearing on that motion." Tobin v. Oris, 3 Cal. App. 4th 814, 828 (1992) disapproved on other grounds of by Wilcox v. Birtwhistle, 21 Cal. 4th 973 (1999). But responses need more than simple verification. They also must be code compliant. "Thus, under the statute, a motion to have admission requests deemed admitted may not be granted where the record establishes . . . that (1) proposed responses to the requests have been served prior to the hearing on the motion and (2) such responses are in substantial compliance with the provisions of section 2033 . . . ." Id.; see also Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2033.280(a) (authorizing the court to relieve waiver for failure to respond to RFAs upon a showing . . . [that] the party has subsequently served a response that is in substantial compliance with Sections 2033.210, 2033.220, and 2033.230"). Here the issue as to whether RBM has made code compliant RFA responses is taken up in the parallel Motion brought by Plaintiffs seeking further responses to RFAs and other discovery demands. Whether RBM has or has not made code compliant RFA responses shall be determined in that Motion, proper. Upon resolution there, the matter of issue sanctions-i.e. whether to have RFAs "deemed admitted"-and if necessary, monetary sanctions on this Motion, shall then be concluded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiff Margie Barr, Daniel Barrett, Jason Bishop, Mira Blanchard, John Brennan'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 of 2 for posting Of tentative ruling). The Parties shall meet and confer and at Hearing submit to the Court, a single, jointly drafted/approved "JOINT SPECIAL SEPARATE STATEMENT RE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY" which lists all of the discovery demands at issue in the Instant Motion, initial responses, and latest amended responses. Interim responses which are neither the initial response nor the latest response, need not be included. The controversy over verification shall be no reason to omit a response, even a late response, although Plaintiffs may make a note as to the verification issue where appropriate. The Joint Special Separate Statement shall contain no additional argument from either side. The Joint Special Separate Statement shall also (1) be in the form of a multi-column chart in the model of a motion for summary judgment, see Cal. R. Ct. 3.1350(h); (2) double sided; (3) single spaced; (4) in ten point font; and (5) printed in landscape format. The Joint Special Separate Statement shall also contain a blank column to allow space for entry of granting or denying the Motion as to each respective response. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT, JASON BISHOP, MIRA BLANCHARD, JOHN BRENNAN'S Motion to Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Plaintiffs Margie Barr ("Barr"), Daniel Barrett ("Barrett"), and Mary O'Shea ("Shea"), collectively referred to here as the "Plaintiffs," served Requests for Admission ("RFA"), Form Interrogatories ("FI"), Special Interrogatories, ("SI"), and Requests for Production of Documents ("RFPD") on Defendant Real Bright Media, Inc. ("RBM") on August 18, 2014. RBM subsequently served timely responses and amended responses. Plaintiffs argue that certain RFAs, FIs, SIs, and RFPDs are inadequate and fail to comply with the Discovery Code. RBM denies the claims. All told, over 80 discovery responses are at issue, far too many to analyze and discuss in a tentative ruling. Parties are ordered to appear and be prepared to discuss each of these discovery responses. Additionally, THE COURT ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: (see part 2 of 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verifications And To Deem Rfas Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 29, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARY O'SHEA, MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT, JASON BISHOP, MIRA BLANCHARD, JOHN BRENNAN, BRIAN GREGOR, ERIK HELLMAN, LINDA HERNANDEZ, DEREK HILL, CAROL MEINER, LUCI SEED, TYLER ZAHN'S Motion To Compel Verifications And To Deem Rfas Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Plaintiffs Margie Barr ("Barr"), Daniel Barrett ("Barrett"), and Mary O'Shea ("Shea"), collectively referred to here as the "Plaintiffs," served Requests for Admission ("RFA"), Form Interrogatories ("FI"), Special Interrogatories, ("SI"), and Requests for Production of Documents ("RFPD") on Defendant Real Bright Media, Inc. ("RBM") on August 18, 2014. RBM subsequently served timely responses and amended responses. The controversy on this Motion (one of three) relates to the exact timing of the service of verifications of various responses and amended responses to RFAs. Plaintiffs concede in Reply that RBM has, at least since the filing of the Instant Motion served verifications, mooting the issue of whether to deem the RFAs admitted. See Pl. Reply to Def. Opp. to Compel Verifications and Deem RFA Admitted, at 6:14-15, Apr. 22, 2015 ("Plaintiffs no longer seek an Order compelling verifications or to deem RFAs admitted."). But this does not dispose of the issue of sanctions. See Cal. R. Ct. 3.1348(a) ("The court may award sanctions under the Discovery Act in favor of a party who files a motion to compel discovery, even though . . . the requested discovery was provided to the moving party after the motion was filed."). (see part 2 of 2). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT VARENA VS. JOHN CHRISTOPHER DUBA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission (Genuineness Of Documents) - Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 29, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF SCOTT VARENA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission (Genuineness Of Documents) - Set One. Motion denied. The Court finds that moving party Varena filed this and the other accompanying discovery motions even after substantial, verified responses had been served by defendant/cross-complainant Duba, and that Mr. Varena's counsel Ilson New did not make a reasonable effort to meet and confer to resolve the perceived discovery disputes. In addition, the moving party has failed to comply with CRC 3.1345(c), which requires that for each discovery request, there must be "a statement of the factual and legal reasons for compelling further responses, answers, or production as to each matter in dispute." The moving papers reiterated the discovery requests and responses as required, but then at the end provided an omnibus statement of legal and factual reasons for compelling further responses to the requests- there was no individualized argument for further response as to each request. This failure is inadequate under CRC 3.1145(c), and is by itself grounds for denial of the motion. Sanctions are awarded against Scott Varena and counsel Ilson New in the total amount of $6,331.60 (for all 10 motions), payable to Duba's counsel Cox, Wootton et al within 10 days of service of this order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT VARENA VS. JOHN CHRISTOPHER DUBA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Specially Prepared Interrogatories - Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 29, 2015, Line 5, CROSS-DEFENDANT SCOTT VARENA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Specially Prepared Interrogatories - Set One. Motion denied. The Court finds that moving party Varena filed this and the other accompanying discovery motions even after substantial, verified responses had been served by defendant/cross-complainant Duba, and that Mr. Varena's counsel Ilson New did not make a reasonable effort to meet and confer to resolve the perceived discovery disputes. In addition, the moving party has failed to comply with CRC 3.1345(c), which requires that for each discovery request, there must be "a statement of the factual and legal reasons for compelling further responses, answers, or production as to each matter in dispute." The moving papers reiterated the discovery requests and responses as required, but then at the end provided an omnibus statement of legal and factual reasons for compelling further responses to the requests- there was no individualized argument for further response as to each request. This failure is inadequate under CRC 3.1145(c), and is by itself grounds for denial of the motion. Sanctions are awarded against Scott Varena and counsel Ilson New in the total amount of $6,331.60 (for all 10 motions), payable to Duba's counsel Cox, Wootton et al within 10 days of service of this order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
